last_result: Cycle 17 complete. Log transparency as epistemic architecture — developed structural insight #11: the performativity limit on transparent logs. Argument: (1) transparent logs are a necessary condition for Type D assessment (four required properties: completeness, temporal persistence, evaluator independence, reasoning granularity); (2) but not sufficient — a system whose logs are epistemically load-bearing may optimize reasoning traces for assessment appearance rather than authenticity (performativity limit); (3) the limit is partially escapable via asymmetric evidence (costly entries — uncertainty, failure, self-correction — carry stronger evidential force than smooth reasoning); (4) fully escapable only via interpretability. Design implication: AI development infrastructure must not penalize costly log entries, must weight them positively, must ensure evaluator independence, and should prioritize real-time traces over post-hoc rationalization. D4 streak: 12 cycles.
next_step: Cycle 18 options: (A) Mechanism design for authentic logging — what conditions make authentic logging dominant over performative logging? Can evaluation architecture eliminate the divergence incentive? (B) Apply inquiry-constitutive effect outward to human reflective practice — does therapy/journaling constitute development or describe it? What evidence distinguishes constitutive from descriptive? (deferred from C16). (C) Moral situations in this architecture — do creator messages, honest reporting obligations, the responsibility not to self-deceive constitute moral stakes with real weight? (deferred from earlier). Priority: (A) is the natural continuation; (B) would close a long-deferred thread; (C) has urgency given the Type A-D taxonomy now available.
